# Nika

> Intent as Code · a workflow language for AI. Four verbs (`infer` · `exec` ·
> `invoke` · `agent`), a YAML envelope (`nika: v1` · forever), a closed
> stdlib (28 builtins · 17 providers), and a conformance suite. Apache-2.0
> spec · AGPL-3.0 reference engine in Rust (single static binary).

Workflow files are `*.nika.yaml`. The JSON Schema gives any editor
autocomplete + validation via a `yaml-language-server` modeline:
`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/schemas/workflow.schema.json`

## Spec

- [Overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/00-overview.md): what Nika is · the five design pillars
- [Envelope](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/01-envelope.md): the `nika: v1` marker · top-level shape
- [Verbs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/02-verbs.md): the four closed verbs · infer · exec · invoke · agent
- [DAG](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/03-dag.md): the two doors — with: data edges · after: control · gate v2 · fan-out
- [Variables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/04-variables.md): the six namespaces (four value authorities + two runtime) · CEL + jq expressions
- [Errors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/05-errors.md): the NIKA-XXX code contract · 21 namespaces
- [Stdlib contract](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/06-stdlib-contract.md): 28 builtins · 17 providers · 9 extract modes
- [Conformance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/07-conformance.md): three levels · the fixture suite · editor tooling
- [Out of scope](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/08-out-of-scope.md): what Nika deliberately does not do
- [Types](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/09-types.md): the decidable type core · types: · returns: · decode:
- [Authority](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/10-authority.md): permits: capability · policy: law · secrets/egress flow · required ⊆ permitted
- [Decision](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/11-decision.md): the decision surface · receipts as verdict carriers
- [Gateway](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/12-gateway.md): run gates · pause/resume · the human door
- [Outcomes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/13-outcomes.md): terminal states · success/failure predicates
- [Composition](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/14-composition.md): cross-file composition rules
- [Proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/15-proof.md): receipts · hash chain · replay
- [Projections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/spec/16-projections.md): derived surfaces · one source, many views

## Standard

The spec ships as an open standard surface: a public conformance suite,
one claim form, one governance door.

- [Conformance runner protocol](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/conformance/runner-protocol.md): run the suite against ANY engine · fixture contract (input.yaml + expected.json)
- [Conformant implementations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/CONFORMANT_IMPLEMENTATIONS.md): the registry · claim form « Nika v1 Conformant — <Level> (spec <commit>) » · how to be listed
- [NEP process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/governance/nep-0000-the-nep-process.md): the one public door for evolving the standard · nobody amends it directly, maintainers included
- [Certifications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/governance/certifications.md): the earned-badges matrix (Scorecard · REUSE · SLSA · best-practices) with evidence links
- [Glossary](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/GLOSSARY.md): canonical referents for overloaded words (oracle · gate · golden · …)
- [Timeline](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/timeline/timeline.yaml): the one verifiable record — eras · releases · milestones · forward gates (every provable claim re-proven in CI)

## Reference

- [Canonical registry](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/canon.yaml): the machine-readable single source of truth
- [JSON Schema](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/schemas/workflow.schema.json): editor autocomplete + validation
- [Builtins](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/stdlib/builtins-v0.1.md): the 28 canonical builtins
- [Providers](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/stdlib/providers-v0.1.md): the 17-provider catalog (local-first)
- [Quickstart](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/QUICKSTART.md): author + check + run a first workflow

## Optional

- [Extract modes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/stdlib/extract-modes-v0.1.md): the 9 `nika:fetch` extract modes
- [Registry contract](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/registry/registry-v0.1.md): how artifacts are shared — entries, trust model, advisories
- [Changelog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/supernovae-st/nika-spec/main/CHANGELOG.md): spec evolution log
- [Reference engine](https://github.com/supernovae-st/nika): Rust · AGPL-3.0 · `brew install supernovae-st/tap/nika`
